tldr: Move from Please's polyglot build targets to Melos workspace scripts when your monorepo is Dart/Flutter rather than multi-language.
why_switch: Melos is purpose-built for Dart and Flutter monorepos and integrates with pub.dev publishing
Smaller config surface than Please for Dart-only workspaces
Native handling of multi-package versioning and changelogs
Active maintenance inside the Flutter plugin ecosystem
Avoids the Bazel-style learning curve when polyglot builds are not needed
what_to_export: List of build targets and their dependencies from BUILD files
export_method: Inventory your Please workspace by walking BUILD files and recording targets, dependencies, and entry points. Capture .plzconfig and any custom rules under build_defs/. Export the package layout (paths, owners, language) into a spreadsheet or YAML you can map to Melos packages. Consult the official Please docs for accurate target/rule semantics before scripting any extractor.
import_method: Initialize Melos at the repo root with a melos.yaml that lists each Dart/Flutter package under packages:. Convert previously declared Please commands into Melos scripts (scripts:) that wrap dart/flutter invocations. For versioning and changelogs, use Melos's built-in version command per the official Melos docs. Re-run analyze/test through melos run to validate parity.

concept_mapping.note: Melos works at package granularity, not per-target
Melos has no rule system; use ecosystem tools
Rely on Dart/Flutter tool caches and CI artifact cache
Bootstrap links local packages and resolves pub deps
Melos covers Dart/Flutter only; keep non-Dart code in a separate build

gotchas: Melos does not provide hermetic, content-addressed builds — expect different reproducibility guarantees
Any non-Dart code in your Please workspace will need a separate build path
No remote build cache equivalent; CI duration may change
Versioning conventions differ — review the Melos versioning docs before first release
Changelog generation depends on conventional commits

email.subject: Migrating our monorepo from Please to Melos
email.body:
Team,

For our Dart/Flutter packages we are moving the build orchestration from Please to Melos. Please was useful for polyglot targets, but our active code is now Dart/Flutter, where Melos is the idiomatic tool: package versioning, changelogs, and pub publishing are first-class.

Plan: inventory current Please targets, map each Dart package into melos.yaml, convert plz commands into Melos scripts, and validate analyze/test parity in CI. Anything non-Dart stays on its own build path for now.

I'll share the migration doc and the cutover window once the dry run passes.

Thanks
